stem: Circle P has a radius of 5 millimeters (mm). Circle Q has an area of 81π mm2. What is the total area, in mm2, of circles P and Q?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The area of a circle with radius r is πr2. For circle P with radius 5, the area is π(5)2 = 25π mm2. Circle Q has an area of 81π mm2. Adding the two areas yields 25π + 81π = 106π mm2. Choices A, B, and D are incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
